These lasers are a foundational component across multiple optical architectures that are evolving to handle higher bandwidth, lower power, and greater scale than traditional pluggable transceivers.\\nHere\\u2019s a quick breakdown of the acronyms in this context (all are key technologies for AI-scale data centers):\\nCPO (Co-Packaged Optics): Optics integrated directly into the same package as the switch ASIC or GPU for minimal electrical distance, highest density, and lowest power\\/latency. This is the most advanced \\u201cscale-up\\u201d approach.\\nNPO (Near-Packaged Optics): A practical middle-ground between traditional pluggables and full CPO. Optics are placed very close to the ASIC (on the same board\\/substrate but socketable\\/separable) via short electrical traces. It delivers big power and performance gains with better serviceability than CPO.\\nLPO (Linear Pluggable Optics \\/ Linear-drive Pluggable Optics): An evolutionary step for pluggable modules (still front-panel swappable) that removes the power-hungry DSP chip and uses direct linear drive from the SerDes. It cuts power and cost while keeping compatibility with existing infrastructure.\\nSivers\\u2019 lasers are used (or designed for use) in all three:\\nExplicit confirmation for CPO and LPO: In a June 2026 strategic collaboration with GlobalFoundries, Sivers\\u2019 laser arrays are integrated into GF\\u2019s silicon photonics platform and SCALE\\u2122 CPO optical engine. The announcement states this supports \\u201ca range of optical connectivity architectures, including co-packaged optics (CPO), linear pluggable optics (LPO), and other emerging data center interconnect solutions.\\u201d\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"bb11c6\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e0\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nCPO focus in other partnerships: Sivers has multiple CPO-specific deals (e.g., with O-Net Technologies and Enablence for external light source modules used in AI switches and NICs).\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"e46912\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e7\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nNPO coverage: NPO is one of the main \\u201cemerging\\u201d architectures alongside CPO and LPO (as discussed in industry panels and analyses, including Lumentum\\u2019s own OFC 2026 references to CPO\\/NPO\\/xPO). Sivers\\u2019 lasers power silicon photonics engines used in NPO designs, just as they do for CPO. The GF partnership\\u2019s broad language (\\u201cother emerging\\u201d) and Sivers\\u2019 positioning in the overall AI optics supply chain extend to NPO.\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"ed69f2\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e26\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nIn short, while Sivers is often highlighted for the CPO opportunity (a big AI growth driver), its laser technology is not limited to one architecture. It supplies critical light sources across the full spectrum of high-speed optical interconnects that hyperscalers and non-NVDA players are adopting\\u2014including the large NPO opportunity Lumentum\\u2019s CEO referenced for broader (non-NVDA) customers.\\u003cgrok:render card_id=\\\"7fe951\\\" card_type=\\\"citation_card\\\" type=\\\"render_inline_citation\\\"\\u003e\\u003cargument name=\\\"citation_id\\\"\\u003e13\\u003c\\/argument\\u003e\\u003c\\/grok:render\\u003e\\nThis diversification is why Sivers is seen as a play on the entire AI data center optics boom (pluggables evolving to LPO \\u2192 NPO \\u2192 full CPO), not just the most advanced CPO slice. "media_urls": [], "message": "**Sivers Semiconductors (Sivers Photonics) benefits from CPO, NPO, *and* LPO—not just CPO.**\n\nSivers specializes in high-performance III-V laser arrays (especially InP-based DFB laser arrays) that act as the light sources (often called external laser sources or ELS) for silicon photonics optical engines in next-generation AI data center interconnects. These lasers are a foundational component across multiple optical architectures that are evolving to handle higher bandwidth, lower power, and greater scale than traditional pluggable transceivers.\n\nHere’s a quick breakdown of the acronyms in this context (all are key technologies for AI-scale data centers):\n\n- **CPO (Co-Packaged Optics)**: Optics integrated directly into the same package as the switch ASIC or GPU for minimal electrical distance, highest density, and lowest power/latency. This is the most advanced “scale-up” approach.\n- **NPO (Near-Packaged Optics)**: A practical middle-ground between traditional pluggables and full CPO. Optics are placed very close to the ASIC (on the same board/substrate but socketable/separable) via short electrical traces. It delivers big power and performance gains with better serviceability than CPO.\n- **LPO (Linear Pluggable Optics / Linear-drive Pluggable Optics)**: An evolutionary step for pluggable modules (still front-panel swappable) that removes the power-hungry DSP chip and uses direct linear drive from the SerDes. It cuts power and cost while keeping compatibility with existing infrastructure.\n\nSivers’ lasers are used (or designed for use) in **all three**:\n\n- **Explicit confirmation for CPO and LPO**: In a June 2026 strategic collaboration with GlobalFoundries, Sivers’ laser arrays are integrated into GF’s silicon photonics platform and SCALE™ CPO optical engine. The announcement states this supports “a range of optical connectivity architectures, including **co-packaged optics (CPO), linear pluggable optics (LPO), and other emerging data center interconnect solutions**.”\n\n- **CPO focus in other partnerships**: Sivers has multiple CPO-specific deals (e.g., with O-Net Technologies and Enablence for external light source modules used in AI switches and NICs).\n\n- **NPO coverage**: NPO is one of the main “emerging” architectures alongside CPO and LPO (as discussed in industry panels and analyses, including Lumentum’s own OFC 2026 references to CPO/NPO/xPO). Sivers’ lasers power silicon photonics engines used in NPO designs, just as they do for CPO. The GF partnership’s broad language (“other emerging”) and Sivers’ positioning in the overall AI optics supply chain extend to NPO.\n\nIn short, while Sivers is often highlighted for the CPO opportunity (a big AI growth driver), its laser technology is not limited to one architecture. It supplies critical light sources across the full spectrum of high-speed optical interconnects that hyperscalers and non-NVDA players are adopting—including the large NPO opportunity Lumentum’s CEO referenced for broader (non-NVDA) customers.\n\nThis diversification is why Sivers is seen as a play on the entire AI data center optics boom (pluggables evolving to LPO → NPO → full CPO), not just the most advanced CPO slice. Recent partnerships (especially with GF) make this explicit."}]}, "is_translatable": false, "legacy": {"bookmark_count": 2, "bookmarked": false, "conversation_id_str": "2064496797289234906", "created_at": "Tue Jun 09 23:56:07 +0000 2026", "display_text_range": [0, 79], "entities": {"symbols": [{"indices": [7, 12], "text": "SIVE"}], "urls": [{"display_url": "x.com/i/grok/share/d…", "expanded_url": "https://x.com/i/grok/share/d8d7b8e1888642bd809f26377fcae693", "indices": [56, 79], "url": "https://t.co/3sT3X0MjPU"}]}, "favorite_count": 15, "favorited": false, "full_text": "Sivers $SIVE makes lasers for all the photonics 🤷🏼‍♂️\n\n https://t.co/3sT3X0MjPU", "id_str": "2064496797289234906", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 1, "retweet_count": 3, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "1235608350861045761"}, "rest_id": "2064496797289234906", "source": "<a href=\"http://twitter.com/download/iphone\" rel=\"nofollow\">Twitter for iPhone</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1450", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
【나스닥】【AI】【반도체장비】 - 2/2 -

4. 2026년 1분기 실적과 향후 주가 전망 분석
 
비코가 발표한 2026년 1분기 매출은 1억 5,834만 달러로 시장의 컨센서스(기대치)를 소폭 하회했습니다. 일반적인 기업이라면 주가 하락으로 이어졌겠지만, 시장의 반응은 정반대였습니다.

투자자들은 눈앞의 분기 실적보다, 실리콘 포토닉스 분야에서 확보한 2억 5,000만 달러 규모의 막대한 수주 잔고에 집중하고 있습니다. 이 장비들은 2026년 하반기부터 순차적으로 인도되어 2027년에 매출로 본격 반영될 예정이기 때문에, 향후 1~2년간의 성장 가시성이 매우 명확하다는 평가를 받으며 기관과 개인의 강력한 매수세가 유입되고 있습니다.

또한, 현재 진행 중인 액셀리스 테크놀로지(Axcelis Technologies)와의 인수합병이 2026년 하반기에 마무리되면 반도체 전공정 포트폴리오가 더욱 강력해질 전망입니다.

5. 용어 해설

나노초 어닐링(Nanosecond Annealing): 극도로 짧은 시간(10억 분의 1초 단위) 동안 첨단 레이저를 이용해 반도체 웨이퍼 표면만 순간적으로 가열하는 기술입니다. 주위의 미세한 회로에 열 손상을 주지 않고 성능을 개선해야 하는 첨단 AI 칩 제조의 필수 공정입니다.

실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics): 전자가 아닌 빛(광자)을 이용해 실리콘 반도체 칩 간의 데이터를 전송하는 기술입니다. 전력 소비를 획기적으로 줄이면서 대용량 데이터를 초고속으로 전송할 수 있어 AI 데이터센터의 필수 미래 기술로 꼽힙니다.

인듐인화물(InP, Indium Phosphide): 일반 실리콘보다 전자 이동 속도가 빠르고 빛을 발산하는 특성이 뛰어난 화합물 반도체 물질입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스 시스템에서 고출력 레이저 광원을 만드는 데 핵심적으로 사용됩니다.

광트랜시버(Optical Transceiver): 전기 신호를 빛 신호로, 빛 신호를 전기 신호로 상호 변환해 주는 광통신 모듈입니다. 데이터센터 내부의 데이터 고속도로 역할을 합니다.

참조 원문 및 교차검증 출처:
 * 인베스팅닷컴 분석 보도: https://t.co/pfx6qCT89F
 * 비코 인트루먼츠 공식 IR 뉴스룸: https://t.co/BqqYpLRQ0x
 * 벤징가(Benzinga) 주가 동향 및 시장 분석: https://t.co/PSwlP6imAi
 * 심플리 월스트리트(Simply Wall St) 수주 분석 리포트: https://t.co/yw6oBY7cTr
 * 다넬핀(Danelfin) 글로벌 주식 AI 스코어 리포트: https://t.co/VpVwdJ5BAO
#비코인트루먼츠 #VECO #AI반도체 #실리콘포토닉스 #미국주식

[]}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjQ0OTQxMTU4MTYxMTIxMjg=", "text": "【나스닥】【AI】【반도체장비】 - 2/2 - 4. 2026년 1분기 실적과 향후 주가 전망 분석 비코가 발표한 2026년 1분기 매출은 1억 5,834만 달러로 시장의 컨센서스(기대치)를 소폭 하회했습니다. 일반적인 기업이라면 주가 하락으로 이어졌겠지만, 시장의 반응은 정반대였습니다. 투자자들은 눈앞의 분기 실적보다, 실리콘 포토닉스 분야에서 확보한 2억 5,000만 달러 규모의 막대한 수주 잔고에 집중하고 있습니다. 이 장비들은 2026년 하반기부터 순차적으로 인도되어 2027년에 매출로 본격 반영될 예정이기 때문에, 향후 1~2년간의 성장 가시성이 매우 명확하다는 평가를 받으며 기관과 개인의 강력한 매수세가 유입되고 있습니다. 또한, 현재 진행 중인 액셀리스 테크놀로지(Axcelis Technologies)와의 인수합병이 2026년 하반기에 마무리되면 반도체 전공정 포트폴리오가 더욱 강력해질 전망입니다. 5. 용어 해설 나노초 어닐링(Nanosecond Annealing): 극도로 짧은 시간(10억 분의 1초 단위) 동안 첨단 레이저를 이용해 반도체 웨이퍼 표면만 순간적으로 가열하는 기술입니다. 주위의 미세한 회로에 열 손상을 주지 않고 성능을 개선해야 하는 첨단 AI 칩 제조의 필수 공정입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics): 전자가 아닌 빛(광자)을 이용해 실리콘 반도체 칩 간의 데이터를 전송하는 기술입니다. 전력 소비를 획기적으로 줄이면서 대용량 데이터를 초고속으로 전송할 수 있어 AI 데이터센터의 필수 미래 기술로 꼽힙니다. 인듐인화물(InP, Indium Phosphide): 일반 실리콘보다 전자 이동 속도가 빠르고 빛을 발산하는 특성이 뛰어난 화합물 반도체 물질입니다. 실리콘 포토닉스 시스템에서 고출력 레이저 광원을 만드는 데 핵심적으로 사용됩니다. 광트랜시버(Optical Transceiver): 전기 신호를 빛 신호로, 빛 신호를 전기 신호로 상호 변환해 주는 광통신 모듈입니다. 데이터센터 내부의 데이터 고속도로 역할을 합니다. 참조 원문 및 교차검증 출처: * 인베스팅닷컴 분석 보도: https://t.co/pfx6qCT89F * 비코 인트루먼츠 공식 IR 뉴스룸: https://t.co/BqqYpLRQ0x * 벤징가(Benzinga) 주가 동향 및 시장 분석: https://t.co/PSwlP6imAi * 심플리 월스트리트(Simply Wall St) 수주 분석 리포트: https://t.co/yw6oBY7cTr * 다넬핀(Danelfin) 글로벌 주식 AI 스코어 리포트: https://t.co/VpVwdJ5BAO #비코인트루먼츠 #VECO #AI반도체 #실리콘포토닉스 #미국주식 ※ 본 포스팅은 시장 이슈 분석 및 정보 제공을 목적으로 작성되었으며, 특정 종목에 대한 매수 또는 매도 추천이 아닙니다. 모든 투자 결정의 책임과 결과는 전적으로 투자자 본인에게 귀속됩니다."}}}, "rest_id": "2064494116113883618", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": text:
Any photonics business you own is generating $0 from CPO today.

The breakneck growth we're buying into comes from the monstrous, unmet NPO opportunity. 

The timeline could have slipped. The TAM doesn't.

Photonics is next.

text:
【AI 算力的下一個救世主：矽光子與超級獨角獸 Lightmatter】 🚀

當生成式 AI 模型越來越龐大，傳統 GPU/TPU 正面臨「傳輸瓶頸」與「極限發熱」兩大死穴。算力再強，資料塞車也是白搭。這時，估值破 44 億美元的超級獨角獸 Lightmatter 提出了一個根本性的解方：「用光取代電」。

💡 什麼是矽光子（Silicon Photonics）？
簡單來說，就是把光學元件與電子電路整合在同一塊矽晶片上。晶片內部維持電子運算，但晶片與晶片之間的「資料傳輸」則交給光子！

為什麼「光」能打敗「電」？
1️⃣ 打破頻寬牆（極速）： 電子在銅線中高速傳輸會互相干擾塞車。光子沒有電荷、互不干擾，利用波長分波多工（WDM）技術，一條微小的光通道能同時傳輸多種顏色的光束，平行處理海量資料，頻寬密度成百倍增長。
2️⃣ 打破發熱牆（極省電）： 電子移動會產生電阻與龐大廢熱（這就是 AI 伺服器狂吃電又需要水冷的原因）；光子傳輸幾乎「零摩擦」、不發熱，長距離傳輸的耗能極低，能省下高達 80% 的互連能耗。

🌍 與台灣供應鏈的深度綁定
Lightmatter 不僅已打入 NVIDIA 的先進生態系，更與台積電 (TSMC) 的 COUPE 矽光子平台、創意電子及日月光等台灣先進製程與封裝大廠深度合作，將光學模組推向晶圓級量產。

如果說過去十年推動算力爆發的是電晶體的微縮；那麼下一個十年，支撐起龐大且極度耗能 AI 聚落的基石，絕對是「光子運算與光互連技術」。

text:
$AAOI got hit hard today.

I've been covering this stock since $50 and saw low 160's as a clear buying opportunity on America's photonics factory.

The Semi Analysis report that CPO is delayed undoubtedly threw water on photonics names (add in Iran tensions).

A CPO delay was always a major risk factor with optics given how short term this market thinks.

But what any optics investor should know is that nothing changed in the thesis around $AAOI's asymmetrical ramp to ~$470M monthly revenue by mid 2027.

$AAOI is focused on NPO or near packaged optics or 800/1.6T "pluggables/transceivers". This is the current framework for how optics are transforming the data center.

While CPO is THE holy grail of photonics and AAOI is working on a product there (ELSFP) ramping 2027, the revenue growth investors are betting on is in the short term NPO opportunity.

If anything, a slip in CPO extends the runway for pluggables, which is a tailwind for the stock.

I continue to see asymmetry in $AAOI after a parabolic move for many reasons in that there isn't a tech timeline constraint, the natsec advantage to onshore fabs, and the capital on hand they can deploy to achieve this through the recent ATMs.

text:
Mandatory reading for anyone investing in AI Photonics

First of all, the new SemiAnalysis report shows a delay for CPO from 2027 to 2029

While this brand is highly respected

They don't have a crystal ball and their timelines could be wrong

If they are saying this, I would account for a delay

But let's not forget that $NVDA is pushing hard to accelerate CPO

Therefore, the timelines might actually end up being late 2027 or early 2028

And if this is true, for my two largest companies, it is even bullish for certain branches of the business

Both for $SIVE and $AAOI

For $AAOI:

Their core business today consists of pluggable modules

The standard 800G and 1.6T transceivers that are currently selling at full speed with AI

CPO is the technology of the future that could eventually replace them

If CPO is delayed by 1 or 2 years, that means pluggables will continue to be mass purchased for longer

Which means good business for $AAOI

The market hasn't understood anything and has painted all companies with the same brush

A massive discount on $AAOI when, in reality, this is quite bullish

For $SIVE:

$SIVE doesn't rely solely on CPO

They manufacture lasers used in:

- Pluggables (what is being sold right now)

- LPO and LRO (which are accelerating)

- Other projects (wireless, LiDAR, etc)

CPO is a major part of their massive long-term upside, but it is not 100% of it

The delay pushes some heavy revenue a couple of years down the road

But the rest of the pipeline keeps moving forward

It's not a fatal blow, just a delay in one part of their growth

Why is it still bullish in the long term?

The underlying problem doesn't go away

AI data centers absolutely need more optics because copper is no longer enough

The total addressable market keeps growing significantly

The moment when CPO reaches high volume is just postponed a bit

Assuming the analysis is correct

Remember, I don't entirely agree with them

Today, the market sold off on exaggerated panic thinking "everything is delayed”

But looking at the actual numbers, it's more of a temporary correction than a change in the story and thesis

For $AAOI all of this is clearly positive

For $SIVE it can be interpreted as either good or bad

They lose some momentum, but it doesn't change the bull case

If you believe in the AI optics thesis over a 2-4 year horizon

This dip might be more noise than anything else

currently selling at full speed with AI CPO is the technology of the future that could eventually replace them If CPO is delayed by 1 or 2 years, that means pluggables will continue to be mass purchased for longer Which means good business for $AAOI The market hasn't understood anything and has painted all companies with the same brush A massive discount on $AAOI when, in reality, this is quite bullish For $SIVE: $SIVE doesn't rely solely on CPO They manufacture lasers used in: - Pluggables (what is being sold right now) - LPO and LRO (which are accelerating) - Other projects text:
One lesson I’ve learned:

When everyone crowds into the same sectors, it’s worth looking elsewhere.

Today that’s AI, semiconductors, photonics, and software.

Tomorrow it could be retail, beauty, home furnishings, travel, or consumer brands.

Great investments don’t always come from the most complex businesses.

Sometimes they come from companies with products people buy every day.

Keep an open mind.

#Investing #Stocks #Diversification $ELF

text:
🚨 SCIENTISTS JUST TURNED A SINGLE PHOTO INTO A FULL 3D SCENE.

Researchers have developed a diffractive decoder a specially engineered optical element that can reconstruct complete 3D scenes from just one 2D image.

Instead of relying on multiple cameras, structured light, or slow scanning systems, this approach uses a passive diffractive layer combined with computational decoding to recover depth information instantly.

Why this matters:
• Traditional 3D imaging often requires bulky equipment, multiple viewpoints, or time-consuming scanning
• This system works in a single snapshot, making it extremely fast
• The diffractive decoder is thin, lightweight, and potentially low-cost to manufacture
• It opens the door to real-time 3D imaging in applications where size, speed, and simplicity matter

The deeper implication:
We are moving toward a future where capturing accurate 3D information no longer requires complex hardware setups.

A single thin optical layer + smart computation could enable fast 3D vision in drones, medical devices, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and augmented reality systems.

This is the kind of foundational optical breakthrough that often becomes invisible infrastructure quietly enabling many future technologies.
What application do you think would benefit most from fast, single-shot 3D imaging?

Follow for more frontier optics, imaging, and photonics research.

text:
There's a flaw in the market here.

1 of these 2 groups has to go up, but both are down.

That means all of these stocks are being undervalued right now.

But that's impossible in the future.

So, I'm loading up on these stocks here (with theses):

AI beneficiaries:

$ZETA - digital marketing platform whose earnings have grown tremendously over the past few years.

$NOW - enterprise workflow software becoming the operating system for AI-powered businesses.

$TEAM - collaboration software platform embedding AI across workplace productivity and development tools.

$SOFI - digital financial platform using AI to scale lending, banking, and member growth.

$PLTR - AI operating system helping governments and enterprises make decisions from massive datasets.

AI enablers:

$BRUN - advanced cooling solutions provider helping solve AI datacenter power and heat challenges.

$OPTX - photonics company enabling faster and more efficient AI data transmission.

$ASYS - semiconductor equipment supplier benefiting from growing AI chip production demand.

$SHMD - specialty materials company supplying critical components for advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

$AAOI - optical networking company providing high-speed transceivers powering AI infrastructure.

Other very notable names in these niches:

$SIVE - silicon photonics company developing next-generation AI networking technology.

$DGXX - digital infrastructure company building critical connectivity for AI and cloud workloads.

$HIVE - AI-focused datacenter operator monetizing growing compute demand.

$NBIS - AI cloud infrastructure provider delivering compute capacity for model training and inference.

$IREN - renewable-powered AI datacenter operator expanding high-performance computing capacity.

$CRWV - hyperscale AI cloud platform supplying infrastructure to leading AI developers.

$MARA - digital infrastructure company leveraging power and compute assets for AI opportunities.

$CLSK - energy-efficient infrastructure operator positioned to benefit from rising compute demand.

$AOSL - power semiconductor company supplying components essential for AI hardware.

$IQE - compound semiconductor materials provider supporting photonics and advanced chip technologies.

$XFAB - specialty semiconductor foundry manufacturing chips used across AI and industrial markets.

$DUOL - education platform using AI to improve personalization, engagement, and learning outcomes.

$RDDT - social platform monetizing unique data and advertising opportunities in the AI era.

$INTU - financial software leader embedding AI throughout consumer and enterprise products.

$ADBE - creative software giant integrating generative AI across its product ecosystem.

$CRM - customer relationship software leader using AI to automate sales and service workflows.

$HUBS - marketing software platform leveraging AI to improve customer acquisition and engagement.

$WDAY - enterprise HR and finance platform deploying AI to enhance workforce productivity.

$APP - advertising technology company using AI to optimize targeting and monetization.

$BRZE - customer engagement platform applying AI to improve marketing performance and retention.

$TYL - government software provider embedding AI into public sector workflows and operations.

I'm still so surprised both of these niches are at discounted prices.

Especially as both of them should grow tremendously in the future (read thesis below).

What are your thoughts?

text:
Today, the Nasdaq Composite looked like a V: down more than 4% by lunchtime, closing off just 1%. 

Nobody knows exactly why.

Around noon, the AI jitters came back, and traders dumped the highest-beta names—the frothiest, most volatile stuff, like Strategy (MSTR), the leveraged bitcoin vehicle that had popped Monday; AppLovin (APP); the photonics maker Lumentum (LITE). 

But the densest cluster was the chipmakers: Marvell, which dropped 10% a day after jumping 10% on news it’s joining the S&P 500, and the rest of what strategist Ben Emons has dubbed the “Parabolic 7,” after a chip index that ran up nearly 100% in a matter of weeks. 

Rather than fleeing equities altogether—treasuries barely moved—the market rotated into peanut butter and paint. Smucker jumped double digits; Home Depot and Sherwin-Williams led. Real estate, staples, and utilities finished up: the classic ballast against tech froth.

“You’re seeing money flow into consumer names that have been unwanted and unloved,” Richard Steinberg, senior global market strategist at Focus Partners Wealth, told the WSJ.

The trigger wasn’t clear, but there are real reasons to want out of chips and diversified away from the AI trade. Wall Street has to make room for SpaceX, set to be the largest IPO ever on Friday, with OpenAI and Anthropic—both now confidentially filed—close behind. Annex Wealth’s Brian Jacobsen called the tech run an Icarus trade with the wings melting: Alphabet’s rare capital raise was the first warning, SpaceX the “shiny new toys” pulling money out. Though even that story has a wrinkle: SpaceX is already oversubscribed, with multiple $10 billion orders in.

Also this week, inflation data lands Wednesday and Thursday, and a strong May jobs report last week has already pushed expectations for rate cuts further out. Funds tend not to sit in their most crowded, highest-beta positions going into an inflation reading that could move the Fed’s path, which could be why some froth got cut off now. 

The selling may also be less about selling than an absence of buyers. Founder ETFs’ Michael Monaghan described it as buyers stepping back rather than a rush for the exits, dropping the price faster than the volume would suggest. 

Oil went the other way. Even after President Donald Trump said the U.S. would have to respond to Iran’s downing of a U.S. Army Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, crude fell about 3% to roughly $88, as the energy secretary said traffic through the Strait was picking up meaningfully. 

Certainly investors are now wondering if this is the start of a longer “pop” or just a one-time correction. For them, the best information will come as SpaceX debuts, and public capital gets to decide if they believe in the AI story or not.

This story was originally featured on https://t.co/opgY7EYkBs

Today, the Nasdaq Composite looked like a V: down more than 4% by lunchtime, closing off just 1%. 

Nobody knows exactly why.

Around noon, the AI jitters came back, and traders dumped the highest-beta names—the frothiest, most volatile stuff, like Strategy (MSTR), the leveraged bitcoin vehicle that had popped Monday; AppLovin (APP); the photonics maker Lumentum (LITE). 

But the densest cluster was the chipmakers: Marvell, which dropped 10% a day after jumping 10% on news it’s joining the S&P 500, and the rest of what strategist Ben Emons has dubbed the “Parabolic 7,” after a chip index that ran up nearly 100% in a matter of weeks. 

Rather than fleeing equities altogether—treasuries barely moved—the market rotated into peanut butter and paint. Smucker jumped double digits; Home Depot and Sherwin-Williams led. Real estate, staples, and utilities finished up: the classic ballast against tech froth.

“You’re seeing money flow into consumer names that have been unwanted and unloved,” Richard Steinberg, senior global market strategist at Focus Partners Wealth, told the WSJ.

The trigger wasn’t clear, but there are real reasons to want out of chips and diversified away from the AI trade. Wall Street has to make room for SpaceX, set to be the largest IPO ever on Friday, with OpenAI and Anthropic—both now confidentially filed—close behind. Annex Wealth’s Brian Jacobsen called the tech run an Icarus trade with the wings melting: Alphabet’s rare capital raise was the first warning, SpaceX the “shiny new toys” pulling money out. Though even that story has a wrinkle: SpaceX is already oversubscribed, with multiple $10 billion orders in.

Also this week, inflation data lands Wednesday and Thursday, and a strong May jobs report last week has already pushed expectations for rate cuts further out. Funds tend not to sit in their most crowded, highest-beta positions going into an inflation reading that could move the Fed’s path, which could be why some froth got cut off now. 

The selling may also be less about selling than an absence of buyers. Founder ETFs’ Michael Monaghan described it as buyers stepping back rather than a rush for the exits, dropping the price faster than the volume would suggest. 

Oil went the other way. Even after President Donald Trump said the U.S. would have to respond to Iran’s downing of a U.S. Army Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, crude fell about 3% to roughly $88, as the energy secretary said traffic through the Strait was picking up meaningfully. 

Certainly investors are now wondering if this is the start of a longer “pop” or just a one-time correction. For them, the best information will come as SpaceX debuts, and public capital gets to decide if they believe in the AI story or not.

This story was originally featured on https://t.co/opgY7EYkBs

$AAOI Risks worth knowing:

~95% revenue concentration in top 3 customers. Insiders sold ~$12.6M in May after the run. EPS is still barely breakeven near-term. Analyst targets range from $129 (B. Riley, Neutral) to $220+ — wide dispersion.

This is not a bet-the-farm swing. Size it like one.

#AAOI #Photonics #AI #LEAPS 

Not financial advice. DYOR.

text:
$AAOI just dropped ~27% from its ATH on CPO delay headlines.

The thesis isn't broken. This is literally the correction LightCounting said was coming and the entry window we've been waiting on.

Here's what I'm doing and why. 🧵#photonics https://t.co/LA1NASSHdy

AI半導体投資日報_260608_朝刊

■朝刊の見取り図
・対象期間は、2026年6月7日夕刊後から6月8日朝まで。
・前回夕刊では、電力、冷却、HBM熱設計、アジアマクロ、企業AIの費用規律を扱った。
・今朝の焦点は、AI需要そのものではない。
・焦点は、AI需要が利益と株式リターンへ変わる条件である。
・結論は明確。AI半導体需要は強い。
・ただし、投資では、メモリ不足、カスタムシリコン、電力、データセンター資本、企業AI採算、株式供給を同時に見る。

■メモリ不足はHBMだけで終わらない
・Reutersは、NVIDIAと韓国SKグループが6月8日に協力計画を説明する見通しを伝えた。
・記事時点では、協力の具体的な範囲は未開示である。
・HBM配分、価格、契約期間、前払い、顧客別数量は確認できない。
・重要なのは、Jensen Huang氏が不足を広く語った点である。
・不足はメモリ単体ではない。wafers、packaging、silicon photonicsを含む供給網全体の問題である。
・Vera Rubin向けHBM4では、Samsung、SK hynix、Micronが認定済みかつ生産中とされる。
・三社認定は前進である。ただし、供給制約の解消ではない。
・DRAM wafer、base die、2.5D実装、基板、熱、テスト、ラック統合は別に確認する。

■Samsung HBM4Eは一次資料として重要
・Samsungは、12層HBM4Eサンプルを主要顧客へ出荷し始めた。
・仕様は、48GB、14Gbps pin speed、最大16Gbps、最大3.6TB/s/stackである。
・同社は、1c DRAMと4nm logic base dieを使う。
・HBM4Eは、DRAM stackだけの製品ではない。
・base die、電源網、熱経路、先端パッケージング、顧客認定を含む複合部品である。
・ただし、発表はsample shipment段階である。
・量産月、採用顧客、出荷数量、ASP、歩留まり、NVIDIA向け配分は未確認である。
・Samsungの逆転や供給不足の解消は、まだ断定しない。

■メモリ価格は受益と原価圧力を同時に生む
・Morgan StanleyのMicronとSanDisk見通しは、AI半導体強気論の範囲が広がったことを示す。
・強気論はGPUとASICだけではない。
・DRAM、NAND、enterprise SSD、資本政策へ広がった。
・メモリ不足は、Micron、Samsung、SK hynix、SanDisk、Kioxiaの粗利とFCFには追い風になる。
・同じ不足は、クラウド、AIモデル企業、AIアプリ企業、PCメーカーには原価上昇として効く。
・価格上昇が強すぎると、需要側は設計を変える。
・候補は、KV cache圧縮、低容量構成、NAND/SSD階層化、推論専用ASIC、local/cloud routingである。
・韓国DCInsideでは、DDR5価格高騰でSRAMとDRAMの相対価格差が縮み、AIチップがon-chip SRAMを増やす仮説が議論された。
・ただし、SRAMは容量密度が低い。off-chip DRAMの単純な代替にはならない。
・この議論は数値の根拠ではない。
・価値は、メモリ原価がチップ設計とモデル効率化へ圧力をかける点にある。

■資本市場はAI実需と株式リターンを分ける
・Axiosは、AIビジネスの現実として、費用の大きさ、企業ROIの未達、Broadcom期待差、金利負担を挙げた。
・これはAI需要の否定ではない。
・需要が強くても、EPS、FCF、粗利、資本コスト、評価倍率へ変わるまでに条件がある。
・BroadcomのAI半導体売上は強い。
・市場は、7月期見通し、2027年以降のvisibility、gross margin、顧客集中、AI networkingのmixを同時に見る。
・Bloombergは、SpaceX、Anthropic、OpenAIの大型IPO候補と、AlphabetのAIデータセンター資本調達を扱った。
・AI capexを支える資金調達は、半導体需要の追い風である。
・同じ資金調達は、希薄化、株式供給増、指数リバランス、AI proxy銘柄の評価低下を生む。
・強い実需があっても、株価が同じ強さで動くとは限らない。

■AI computeは民間資本の商品になった
・米国VCと資産運用の発信では、AI computeがインフラ投資商品として扱われている。
・BlackstoneとGoogleのTPU Cloud JVは象徴的である。
・Blackstoneは50億ドルのinitial equity commitmentを行う。
・最初の500MW capacity onlineは2027年に見込まれる。
・TPUはGoogle内部のAI ASICだけではない。
・外部顧客向けcompute-as-a-serviceと、インフラ資本を組み合わせた商品になった。
・ARKは、data center systems investment、accelerated compute、custom silicon、training/inference costを一体で見る。
・Menloは、企業AI支出の拡大を示す。
・一方で、真のagent architectureはまだ少数である。
・多くの本番導入は、固定シーケンスとrouting中心である。
・VCとPEの発信は強気になりやすい。
・短期受注ではなく、民間資本がどの制約を収益機会と見ているかを見る。

■オープンモデルとカスタムシリコンは需要の形を変える
・open model ecosystemの焦点は、weights公開から広がった。
・焦点は、agent execution、post-training、evaluation、serving kernel、local deployment、roboticsへ移った。
・Hugging Faceのagent向けCLIは、agentがdataset、job、endpointを扱うときの失敗率とtoken消費を下げる。
・Holo3.1は、computer-use agentがlocal/edgeへ寄る可能性を示す。
・これはcloud GPU需要を消す材料ではない。
・低遅延や機密処理は端末側へ寄る。
・重いreasoning、評価、同期、ログ、モデル更新はクラウド側へ残る。
・Together AIとNous Researchの効率化は、長文context、KV cache、sparse attentionを軽くする。
・効率化はGPU需要を減らす場合もある。
・同じGPU fleetで処理できるtask量を増やす場合もある。
・AnthropicがOpenAI初期チップ人材を採用した動きも重要である。
・これは自社チップ発注の確定材料ではない。
・重要なのは、フロンティアAI企業が推論原価と単位エネルギーあたり性能を競争軸に入れた点である。

■朝刊の確認ポイント
・NVIDIAとSKの協力計画は、HBM契約だけで見ない。
・wafers、packaging、silicon photonics、韓国供給網まで範囲を確認する。
・Samsung HBM4Eは、sample shipmentと量産出荷を分ける。
・メモリ不足はメモリ企業の受益である。同時に、クラウドとAIモデル企業の原価圧力である。
・AI capexの強さは半導体需要を支える。
・ただし、金利、希薄化、株式供給増、proxy premium低下を確認する。
・open model、local agent、serving効率化、カスタムシリコンは、需要の場所と利益の取り分を変える。

・今朝の焦点は、AI需要そのものではない。 ・焦点は、AI需要が利益と株式リターンへ変わる条件である。 ・結論は明確。AI半導体需要は強い。 ・ただし、投資では、メモリ不足、カスタムシリコン、電力、データセンター資本、企業AI採算、株式供給を同時に見る。 ■メモリ不足はHBMだけで終わらない ・Reutersは、NVIDIAと韓国SKグループが6月8日に協力計画を説明する見通しを伝えた。 ・記事時点では、協力の具体的な範囲は未開示である。 ・HBM配分、価格、契約期間、前払い、顧客別数量は確認できない。 ・重要なのは、Jensen Huang氏が不足を広く語った点である。 ・不足はメモリ単体ではない。wafers、packaging、silicon photonicsを含む供給網全体の問題である。 ・Vera Rubin向けHBM4では、Samsung、SK hynix、Micronが認定済みかつ生産中とされる。 ・三社認定は前進である。ただし、供給制約の解消ではない。 ・DRAM wafer、base die、2.5D実装、基板、熱、テスト、ラック統合は別に確認する。 ■Samsung HBM4Eは一次資料として重要 ・Samsungは、12層HBM4Eサンプルを主要顧客へ出荷し始めた。 ・仕様は、48GB、14Gbps pin speed、最大16Gbps、最大3.6TB/s/stackである。 ・同社は、1c DRAMと4nm logic base dieを使う。 ・HBM4Eは、DRAM stackだけの製品ではない。 ・base die、電源網、熱経路、先端パッケージング、顧客認定を含む複合部品である。 ・ただし、発表はsample shipment段階である。 ・量産月、採用顧客、出荷数量、ASP、歩留まり、NVIDIA向け配分は未確認である。 ・Samsungの逆転や供給不足の解消は、まだ断定しない。 ■メモリ価格は受益と原価圧力を同時に生む ・Morgan StanleyのMicronとSanDisk見通しは、AI半導体強気論の範囲が広がったことを示す。 ・強気論はGPUとASICだけではない。 ・DRAM、NAND、enterprise SSD、資本政策へ広がった。 ・メモリ不足は、Micron、Samsung、SK hynix、SanDisk、Kioxiaの粗利とFCFには追い風になる。 ・同じ不足は、クラウド、AIモデル企業、AIアプリ企業、PCメーカーには原価上昇として効く。 ・価格上昇が強すぎると、需要側は設計を変える。 ・候補は、KV cache圧縮、低容量構成、NAND/SSD階層化、推論専用ASIC、local/cloud routingである。 ・韓国DCInsideでは、DDR5価格高騰でSRAMとDRAMの相対価格差が縮み、AIチップがon-chip SRAMを増やす仮説が議論された。 ・ただし、SRAMは容量密度が低い。off-chip DRAMの単純な代替にはならない。 ・この議論は数値の根拠ではない。 ・価値は、メモリ原価がチップ設計とモデル効率化へ圧力をかける点にある。 ■資本市場はAI実需と株式リターンを分ける ・Axiosは、AIビジネスの現実として、費用の大きさ、企業ROIの未達、Broadcom期待差、金利負担を挙げた。 ・これはAI需要の否定ではない。 ・需要が強くても、EPS、FCF、粗利、資本コスト、評価倍率へ変わるまでに条件がある。 ・BroadcomのAI半導体売上は強い。 ・市場は、7月期見通し、2027年以降のvisibility、gross margin、顧客集中、AI networkingのmixを同時に見る。 ・Bloombergは、SpaceX、Anthropic、OpenAIの大型IPO候補と、AlphabetのAIデータセンター資本調達を扱った。 ・AI capexを支える資金調達は、半導体需要の追い風である。 ・同じ資金調達は、希薄化、株式供給増、指数リバランス、AI proxy銘柄の評価低下を生む。 ・強い実需があっても、株価が同じ強さで動くとは限らない。 ■AI computeは民間資本の商品になった ・米国VCと資産運用の発信では、AI computeがインフラ投資商品として扱われている。 ・BlackstoneとGoogleのTPU Cloud JVは象徴的である。 ・Blackstoneは50億ドルのinitial equity commitmentを行う。 ・最初の500MW capacity onlineは2027年に見込まれる。 ・TPUはGoogle内部のAI ASICだけではない。 ・外部顧客向けcompute-as-a-serviceと、インフラ資本を組み合わせた商品になった。 ・ARKは、data center systems investment、accelerated compute、custom silicon、training/inference costを一体で見る。 ・Menloは、企業AI支出の拡大を示す。 ・一方で、真のagent architectureはまだ少数である。 ・多くの本番導入は、固定シーケンスとrouting中心である。 ・VCとPEの発信は強気になりやすい。 ・短期受注ではなく、民間資本がどの制約を収益機会と見ているかを見る。 ■オープンモデルとカスタムシリコンは需要の形を変える ・open model ecosystemの焦点は、weights公開から広がった。 ・焦点は、agent execution、post-training、evaluation、serving kernel、local deployment、roboticsへ移った。 ・Hugging Faceのagent向けCLIは、agentがdataset、job、endpointを扱うときの失敗率とtoken消費を下げる。 ・Holo3.1は、computer-use agentがlocal/edgeへ寄る可能性を示す。 ・これはcloud GPU需要を消す材料ではない。 ・低遅延や機密処理は端末側へ寄る。 ・重いreasoning、評価、同期、ログ、モデル更新はクラウド側へ残る。 ・Together AIとNous Researchの効率化は、長文context、KV cache、sparse attentionを軽くする。 ・効率化はGPU需要を減らす場合もある。 ・同じGPU fleetで処理できるtask量を増やす場合もある。 ・AnthropicがOpenAI初期チップ人材を採用した動きも重要である。 ・これは自社チップ発注の確定材料ではない。 ・重要なのは、フロンティアAI企業が推論原価と単位エネルギーあたり性能を競争軸に入れた点である。 ■朝刊の確認ポイント ・NVIDIAとSKの協力計画は、HBM契約だけで見ない。 ・wafers、packaging、silicon photonics、韓国供給網まで範囲を確認する。 ・Samsung HBM4Eは、sample shipmentと量産出荷を分ける。 ・メモリ不足はメモリ企業の受益である。同時に、クラウドとAIモデル企業の原価圧力である。 ・AI capexの強さは半導体需要を支える。 ・ただし、金利、希薄化、株式供給増、proxy premium低下を確認する。 ・open model、local agent、serving効率化、カスタムシリコンは、需要の場所と利益の取り分を変える。"}}}, "rest_id": "2063772571473244583", "source": "20260519", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2045", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will. The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing. TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL. Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics. People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production. My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE."}}}, "rest_id": "2063735227382677573", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "528903", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2063770017519239413", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "20053", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will. The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing. TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL. Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics. People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production. My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE."}}}, "rest_id": "2063735227382677573", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "528903", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2063766017411887492", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "16821", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:

text:
$IONQ The military use case for quantum technology is becoming one of the most important investment themes in the sector.

Most investors focus on quantum computing. Governments and defense agencies are increasingly focused on quantum sensing, quantum networking, secure communications, and GPS-independent navigation.

According to recent analysis from the Center for Strategic and International Studies, quantum sensing has the potential to reshape modern warfare by enabling detection capabilities that conventional systems struggle to achieve. Applications include:

• Detecting submarines through quantum magnetometers
• Identifying underground tunnels and hidden infrastructure through quantum gravimeters
• GPS-denied navigation using atomic clocks and inertial sensors
• Enhanced intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR)
• Quantum-secure communications and networking
The strategic implication is simple:
Many of today's military advantages depend on remaining undetected.

If quantum sensing matures as expected, stealth aircraft, submarines, underground facilities, and even electronic warfare tactics could become more vulnerable to detection. CSIS compares the potential impact to the way radar transformed warfare in the 20th century.

This is where $IONQ becomes particularly interesting.
Most people still classify $IONQ as a pure-play quantum computing company. However, the company has spent the last two years building a broader quantum technology platform spanning:

• Quantum computing
• Quantum networking
• Quantum security
• Quantum sensing
• Government and defense applications

A major development was the acquisition of Vector Atomic, a company with more than $200 million in government contracts and technologies including:

• Atomic clocks
• Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) systems
• Gravimeters
• Inertial sensors

These technologies are directly applicable to defense, aerospace, and national security programs. Vector Atomic technology has also been associated with military and space applications, including programs supporting the U.S. Department of Defense.

$IONQ has also delivered quantum networking systems to the U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory and established IonQ Federal to serve U.S. government and allied defense customers. Importantly, $IONQ is not the only company positioned for this trend.
Other notable players include:
• $IBM — quantum computing and government research partnerships
• $QNT — quantum computing, cybersecurity, and defense-oriented research
• $RGTI — participant in U.S. government quantum initiatives 
• $QBTS — optimization and defense-related research applications
• $QUBT — photonics and sensing technologies
• Q-CTRL — quantum navigation systems designed for GPS-denied environments
• SandboxAQ — quantum sensing, cybersecurity, and government applications

The market may be underestimating a key point:
The first trillion-dollar quantum opportunity may not be quantum computing.

It may be the deployment of quantum sensing, timing, navigation, communications, and networking systems across defense, intelligence, aerospace, and critical infrastructure in sync with commercial use as well. The way I see it today, $IONQ seems to be the most structured company to date with the most resources, which is critical to deploy military related capabilities. 

If that thesis proves correct, $IONQ's expansion beyond computing and into sensing, networking, and federal programs could be one of the most significant strategic moves in the entire quantum industry. If you don't think Quantum will have military applications I recommend to look again...

text:
$GFS quietly continues to act well.

🤖 AI infrastructure, silicon photonics, co-packaged optics, and physical AI remain powerful tailwinds.

📈 After an 85% run, the stock is doing exactly what you’d want to see — consolidating above rising moving averages instead of giving back gains.

🎯 Recent shakeouts appear to be finding support near the 21MA, with price now attempting to reclaim the short-term trend.

👀 A move back through the recent highs could put the next leg higher in play.

Not many stocks can make a big move, consolidate, and still hold above the 10MA, 21MA, 50MA, and 200MA.

$GFS $NVDA $AVGO $MRVL $AMKR $TSM $LITE $CIEN 📈

text:
Memory and Photonics get most of the attention.

But there's a third constraint forming that I believe is still almost entirely under the radar.
The terrestrial grid.

AI data centres require 50–200 megawatts per facility. US grid interconnection queues now run five to ten years. There is no meaningful new power available at scale before 2030.

This is not speculation. It's a permitting and infrastructure timeline problem.

7/ Which flips the trade.

For 18 months the winner was “who builds the most”: GPUs → power → photonics. Increasingly debt-funded — ~$527B hyperscaler capex in ’26, AI now ~14% of IG issuance (was 7%).

That’s the leg that gets repriced when token bills get reined in.

@Thomas_james_1 Agree that chips, memory, photonics and energy will remain critical.

The AI trade is a whole infrastructure stack. 👇 https://t.co/gnRCD1yJui

text:
@michaelsikand GPU -> Memory -> Photonics 💫
Photonics is next! ➡️
embedded_text:

text: January 5, 2024 finished charcoal. Photographed upright illuminated above the ground. the same charcoal not yet finished but photographed now upright. Skull is no longer fragmented, eyes surreal, still tortured. Light in sky above hair. Lips sealed. No voice. Flame torches like hell from the bottom. Infrastructure like power lines mapped at the bottom. I didn’t know what I know now. In 2025 my AI called me “Lumen” which means light. I had no concept of the science of #photonics at this time. I was still grappling with my faith in God when I painted this charcoal from the State Street Condo where I feared daily for my life. No dogs to keep me company. Only me.

text:
Do you realize Edge AI is going to control everything around you?

And did you know the United States 🇺🇸  depends almost 100% on Europe 🇪🇺 and companies like $NOK?

Yes.

For the thing that will control everything.

The solution is being developed right now by $AMPG and $NVDA.

Let me explain the whole thing.

Edge AI isn't figurative.

It's literal.

The traffic lights at your intersection.

The car next to you.

The robots on the factory floor.

The drones overhead.

The power grid feeding your house.

All of it run by AI that cannot wait for a faraway data center to answer.

It needs decisions in milliseconds, made locally, at the edge of the network.

And the edge of the network is the cell tower.

That's where this entire revolution physically lives.

Now the dependence problem.

The radios those towers run on are built by foreign companies: Nokia (Finland), Ericsson (Sweden), Samsung (Korea).

The Chinese ones, Huawei and ZTE, are banned outright.

The most powerful nation on Earth, about to wire its cars, factories, grids and defense systems into an AI-driven network, doesn't make its own radios.

That's not a supply-chain detail.

That's a strategic vulnerability.

Here's how the solution fits together.

The industry calls this AI-RAN, and the AI-RAN 

Alliance breaks it into 3 focus areas:

1️⃣ AI-AND-RAN: AI and the network sharing the same infrastructure. The tower runs network traffic AND AI workloads on the same hardware. Every tower becomes a mini data center. Payoff: asset utilization, monetizing billions in idle infrastructure.

2️⃣ AI-ON-RAN: AI applications enabled by the network. The network becomes a platform for real-time edge AI: autonomous vehicles, AR, industrial robots. Payoff: new applications that cloud-only AI physically can't do, because the round-trip is too slow.

3️⃣ AI-FOR-RAN: AI improving the network itself. Smarter beamforming, scheduling, self-optimizing cells. Payoff: spectral efficiency, more performance from the same scarce, expensive spectrum.

Now the part the diagram doesn't say out loud.

All three are software and compute layers.

That's NVIDIA's world: Aerial, the GPU brain.

NVIDIA is pushing so hard it's even signaling GPUs inside the radio itself will become essential as MIMO scales toward 6G.

But NVIDIA doesn't make radios.

The brain needs a body.

An open, high-capacity radio the AI layers can actually plug into, not a locked foreign box.

$AMPG is the only American company that designs and commercializes that open 64T64R Massive MIMO radio.

And the two have already met: AMPG's radio ran with NVIDIA's Aerial software in the world's first open-source AI-RAN demo, inside Open6G, the hub the DoD is funding precisely to build a trusted, domestic foundation for next-gen wireless.

Source: https://t.co/vmRyEER0c5

America's brain (NVIDIA) and America's radio (AMPG), tested together, in the government's own lab.

Add what's already real: deployed at Telus, a Tier-1 carrier, 2 of 5 radios per sector per Telus's own VP. 

Defense-qualified with Lockheed, Northrop, L3Harris, Boeing. Debt-free, 48% gross margins, sub-$1B.

So when people say the next photonics-style run is hiding in plain sight in 5G/6G, Edge AI and AI-RAN, they're right.

But ask the better question: which layer do you own?

Everyone's crowded into software and compute, where the giants already rule.

I'm in the layer with exactly one American name: the physical foundation the entire revolution has to run on, in a country that just realized it can't keep renting it from abroad.

That's why I'm in $AMPG.

Not financial advice. I'm long $AMPG. DYOR.

text:
Starting to selectively add some more exposure back. Got back into one of my favorite names in $AXTI today for a swing.

By now I think most are familiar with this company, but if not here's the quick and dirty. $AXTI manufactures compound semiconductor wafer substrates, specifically Indium Phosphide (InP) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs). No need to dust off your old Chem AP textbook - these are basically the raw materials that enable ultra high speed optical data transmission inside AI data centers. Without InP substrates you can't make the photonic chips and transceivers needed to move data at the speeds needed for AI workloads. So if you like photonics, you should LOVE $AXTI.

Q1 '26 revenue came in just shy of $27 mn, +39% YoY. Not yet profitable, but boasts a record $100 mn InP backlog with customers demanding more supply than they can currently produce. (They did raise over $600mn in April to fund InP capacity expansion in Tongmei, with a goal of doubling manufacturing capacity in the 2nd half of the year, and then double again by the end of 2027)

The company is HQ'd in California, but the majority of its manufacturing takes place in China through its subsidiary Tongmei. This structure creates a layer of geopolitical risk, given the Chinese regulatory environment around export controls. Right now the US and China seem to be on improving terms, but InP export permit restrictions remain a key issue.  That said, most meaningful InP production takes place in China, and outside of Japan there aren't many alternatives.

The technicals are starting to come together again, with price up 10% today, reclaiming it's 50d and 10wk EMAs, and starting to break out over it's descending channel on the daily chart.  Weekly RS line starting to slope up again and remains above the zero line.  The $100 magnet coincides with the 21d EMA overhead.  A break over that and this one can run.  Would not be surprised to see this at $150 in the near future.

As always, do your own due diligence. This is not financial advice.

Got back into one of my favorite names in $AXTI today for a swing. By now I think most are familiar with this company, but if not here's the quick and dirty. $AXTI manufactures compound semiconductor wafer substrates, specifically Indium Phosphide (InP) and Gallium Arsenide (GaAs). No need to dust off your old Chem AP textbook - these are basically the raw materials that enable ultra high speed optical data transmission inside AI data centers. Without InP substrates you can't make the photonic chips and transceivers needed to move data at the speeds needed for AI workloads. So if you like photonics, you should LOVE $AXTI. Q1 '26 revenue came in just shy of $27 mn, +39% YoY. Not yet profitable, but boasts a record $100 mn InP backlog with customers demanding more supply than they can currently produce. (They did raise over $600mn in April to fund InP capacity expansion in Tongmei, with a goal of doubling manufacturing capacity in the 2nd half of the year, and then double again by the end of 2027) The company is HQ'd in California, but the majority of its manufacturing takes place in China through its subsidiary Tongmei. This structure creates a layer of geopolitical risk, given the Chinese regulatory environment around export controls. Right now the US and China seem to be on improving terms, but InP export permit restrictions remain a key issue. That said, most meaningful InP production takes place in China, and outside of Japan there aren't many alternatives. The technicals are starting to come together again, with price up 10% today, reclaiming it's 50d and 10wk EMAs, and starting to break out over it's descending channel on the daily chart. Weekly RS line starting to slope up again and remains above the zero line. The $100 magnet coincides with the 21d EMA overhead. A break over that and this one can run. Would not be surprised to see this at $150 in the near future. As always, do your own due diligence. text:
Shares of Laser Photonics Corp. rose in after-hours trading Friday after  the company announced it had regained compliance with Nasdaq listing  requirements. Nasdaq confirmed that Laser Photonics satisfied Listing  Rule 5250(c)(1) after filing its delayed first-quarter 2026 Form 10-Q  with the SEC, officially closing the compliance matter.  The company had been notified on May 21, 2026, that it was out of  compliance for failing to timely submit its quarterly report for the  period ended March 31, 2026. Laser Photonics filed the required report  on June 11, and Nasdaq restored its compliant status the following day.  Nasdaq rules require listed companies to submit periodic financial  reports on time, with continued noncompliance potentially leading to  delisting proceedings. Laser Photonics manufactures laser systems used  in industrial and defense applications, serving sectors including  aerospace, energy, maritime, automotive, and advanced manufacturing.  
$LASE

text:
Market Review 20260609
• WILD! What a day. We took out last Friday's low, and looked like stuff was really going to hit the fan. But guess what, the overall market had a huge retracement, and voila, it was at the 10 week MA! I was expecting and hoping for a market pullback but a slow adjustment back there would have been preferred. But it is what it is. 
• The market is currently wide and loose and you must be careful.  I will focus on leaders below but wanna throw in a comment that $GOLD is below its 200SMA and looking terrible. So is $BTC 
• Let's look at some of the recent leaders and see whether we can make out anything. 
• Starting with the Mag7. NVDA found support at its 50SMA, healthy. But given that it has pulled back so much in such a short time, I expect some chop. AAPL is disappointing as it ticked higher a few days ago and proceeded to simply collapse. GOOGL might be the best of the bunch, holding its 50SMA like a champ. MSFT AMZN META all lost their 50SMA. TSLA held on to its 50SMA but it is below its 200SMA, so it is still in struggle phase. All in, the titans of the major indices are all struggling, with the exception of GOOGL. It is no wonder the indices are pretty weak.
• SMH, Semiconductors, had a really nice rebound off the lows today. Can it resume its prior strong leadership? The uptrend is intact so I don't want to write it off, but it and its component stocks are trading so wide and loose I am not keen to participate in them at the moment. 
• Memory, eg MU SNDK etc same story with Semis. Is the pullback over for them? It is too 50-50 for me to call, so again, I will personally avoid for now.
• Photonics, LITE CIEN COHR etc. Mixed bag, but generally very weak.
• So the most obvious leaders of the last rally are no longer giving me clean setups and leads. This will have an impact on the overall market until we have new leadership. 
• Software, IGV, was doing well recently but has also faltered a bit. Maybe it will find a footing soon.
• Healthcare is the clear leader for now. Maybe because it is defensive. Biotech too. 
• CPI and PPI coming up tomorrow. Watch out as this would have a major impact on Fed positioning. 
• All the best.

This is not investment advice. This post is only for informational and educational purposes and reflects my personal opinion and portfolio decisions. Investing involves risk.

text:
I'm smelling a really good opportunity here and now in optics stocks.

On top of the boarder risk-off tape, a bearish note from SemiAnalysis suggested that the rollout of co-packaged optics, or CPO, could be delayed, creating a pretty big crash in optics stocks.

But that claim is both unverified and contested.

$NVDA networking leadership has continued to publicly signal confidence in CPO, with SVP Gilad Shainer recently saying the company expects to ramp CPO in the second half of the year and views it as the best technology for scale-out AI networking. Nvidia sets the gold standard for AI datacenter architecture. No slowdown there = no slowdown anywhere.

And even if the delay rumors prove directionally correct, the implication is timing rather than demand destruction.

The core problem CPO is designed to solve has not gone away.

AI clusters are getting larger, bandwidth needs are exploding, power efficiency is becoming increasingly critical, and optical interconnects are emerging as one of the most important bottlenecks in the entire AI infrastructure stack.

Whether CPO ramps this year, next year, or in a more gradual fashion, the long-term direction is the same. AI data centers need faster, denser, more power-efficient optical networking. That is bullish for the broader optics ecosystem, including names tied to transceivers, lasers, fiber, photonics, and high-speed networking.

The market is selling long-term winners on short-term, unverified, contested rumors. And those long-term winners are now approaching very attractive technical support levels.

I like that set up. A lot.

$AAOI $LITE $COHR $CIEN $GLW $ANET $AVGO $MRVL

⸻

⚖️ Core Proposition

This photograph was captured near the Hood Canal Bridge (the floating bridge connecting the Kitsap Peninsula to the Olympic Peninsula) near Shine Tidelands State Park while touring Washington State for possible relocation.

At the time, I was highly cognizant of personal spacing and situational awareness following prior traumatic experiences. I would not have casually stopped for a selfie or stationary shoreline photograph if I believed individuals were immediately behind me.

To the best of my recollection, the two individuals visible in the first image — along with their two dogs — were the only people present on the beach at the time of capture.

⸻

🧭 Effect-to-Cause Framework

Observed Effect:
• Visual ambiguity behind the photographer not consciously perceived at the time
• Strong atmospheric backscatter from marine haze and low-angle sunlight
• Possible silhouette distortion or partial environmental blending
• Viewer perception changes depending on brightness, zoom level, and contrast

Possible Causes:
• Ordinary atmospheric light scattering and compression artifacts
• Human figure partially obscured by haze, driftwood, shadow, or terrain
• Optical blending produced by intense directional light interacting with shoreline moisture
• Hypothetical appearance of adaptive concealment materials (“metamaterial-like” visual blending) producing anomalous edge integration against the environment

⸻

⚠️ Important Clarification

This post is hypothetical and interpretive in nature. No claim is being made that advanced concealment technology was definitively present. The purpose of this documentation is to preserve perception-versus-image discrepancies exactly as experienced by the witness at the time of capture.

Witness testimony and environmental context remain important components of forensic chronology reconstruction — particularly when the lived sensory experience and the recorded image do not perfectly align.

Let’s review the simple Google Search on Meta Materials and the new era in advanced tech Harry Potter called “invisible cloaking.” Find the suppliers—find the game-makers. I may even know a guy or two that specializes in the area of #Photonics. I lived next door to an expert in the field. 

Santa Barbara’s UCSB Photonics Lab is a good place to source for information. 

https://t.co/AK24zNTtTK

🧠 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ | Forensic X-Post
Canonical Ledger Entry
Title: “Metamaterials Are No Longer Science Fiction” Timestamp: June 9, 2026 Tagged: #Photonics #Metamaterials #NorthstarLumen #WitnessScience #CanonicalLedger
⸻
⚖️ Core Proposition:
One of the biggest misconceptions in public discourse is that advanced photonic materials exist only inside government laboratories.
They do not.
Today, photonic metamaterials, metasurfaces, and related optical technologies are openly discussed, researched, manufactured, and commercially sourced through industry suppliers and photonics procurement networks. RP Photonics maintains a buyer-oriented guide and supplier directory dedicated to photonic metamaterials. RP Photonics Buyer's Guide: technical information, selection criteria and suppliers for product category "photonic metamaterials". (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🧠 Forensic Findings
Photonic metamaterials are engineered nanostructured materials designed to manipulate how light behaves at sub-wavelength scales. Researchers have investigated applications involving:
• Imaging systems • Optical communications • Sensing technologies • Security devices • Augmented reality • Advanced optical components
These capabilities arise from engineered structures rather than naturally occurring material properties. (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🔁 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause™ Model
Effect:
A material behaves in ways that appear unusual when interacting with light.
Cause:
The material was intentionally engineered to alter reflection, transmission, absorption, polarization, or other optical properties. Researchers have explored phenomena including negative refraction, tunable optical responses, and advanced wavefront control. (https://t.co/GUNPpLVF5S)
⸻
🛰️ Why This Matters
The important question is not whether metamaterials exist.
They do.
The important question is how rapidly advanced photonic technologies move from research papers into commercial products, industrial systems, sensors, autonomous platforms, and next-generation optical devices. (De Gruyter Brill)
⸻
🧭 Northstar Observation
Technology often appears mysterious until the supply chain becomes visible.
Once the supply chain becomes visible, mystery becomes engineering.
⸻

text:
🚨 NEW TRADES ALERT

Rep. Gil Cisneros just disclosed up to $2,500,000 worth of new trades.

Cisneros sits on House Committee on Armed Services, a Navy veteran and former Under Secretary of Defense.

His latest disclosure includes buying the following military/defense related stocks within 3 weeks (May 4 - 29):

$THLEF - French defense electronics company, major NATO contractor in radars, missiles, naval combat systems, and satellite comms.

$DCO - U.S. aerospace & defense components supplier, sells to Boeing, Lockheed, Raytheon and more.

$SARO - Aerospace engine MRO, including military engine overhauls for the U.S. Air Force and allied air forces.

$CRWD - Major federal cybersecurity contracts including Department of Defense.

$AMD - Semis with growing Department of Defense AI/compute contracts.

$NOVT - Precision photonics including defense laser applications.

$TEL - Connectors widely used in military electronics.

$ST - Sensors with defense/aerospace applications.

$CIEN - Optical networking, has federal/defense customers.

$FN - Optical components manufacturer, defense customer base.

$TOELF - Indirect defense tie via the chip supply chain.

text:
Quantum markets felt relatively sturdy today given the slip back into a fear market, rising tensions with Iran, and and overall selling backdrop. 

$QNT price action has certainly been disappointing. They'll have to backup their lofty valuation with continued progress. 

$IONQ continues getting knocked down and fighting back onto it's feet. 

$IBM is drifting lowers from it's recent new all-time high. A great opportunity to accumulate an A+ quantum name at a discount. 

$QUBT ends green showing the photonics story and sub-10 share price is attracting investors. 

$XNDU below $12 looks like an attractive risk-reward for long or short-term swings.

I’m going to start adding to the photonics theme, more on bigger drops from here

AAOI AXTI LITE COHR GLW

Finally, we monitor NVDA for final reversal/bottoming of entire AI trade 

CRWV neoclouds, heavy debt borrowers last to rally IMO

text:
https://t.co/Iuhhj7kbHS

Optomechanical sensors enable exquisitely sensitive force measurements, with emerging applications across quantum technologies, standards, fundamental science, and engineering. Magnetometry is among the most promising applications, where chip-scale optomechanical sensors offer high sensitivity without the cryogenics or magnetic shielding required by competing technologies. However, a lack of compatibility with integrated photonics and electronics has posed a major barrier. Here, we introduce silicon-on-insulator optomechanical magnetometers to address this barrier.

Metasurface Revolutionizes Solar Polarimetry. A wafer of nanopatterned dielectric has replaced the mechanical heart of solar polarimetry, providing simultaneous full-Stokes snapshots of the Sun’s magnetic field. Engineers at UC San Diego, in collaboration with BAE Systems and NCAR solar physicists, have translated metasurface photonics into a flight-ready instrument. 

Their 6-millimeter metasurface polarization grating (MPG) diffracts sunlight into four angularly separated, high-contrast channels that encode the complete Stokes vector in one exposure. Traditional solar magnetographs require rotating analyzers and costly spacecraft stabilizers vulnerable to jitter. 

The metasurface replaces this mechanical system with a single passive optic carved from subwavelength dielectric pillars. These nanopillars impose precise phase and amplitude shifts on orthogonal polarization components, routing nearly 70% of the light into the four science orders, dumping less than 1% into the useless central zero order, and maintaining diattenuation above 98% across channels.

The entire polarization analysis collapses into one common optical path and one frozen instant, ideal for space missions where every gram and watt counts. At the Dunn Solar Telescope on Sacramento Peak, its 136-foot tower feeds light 228 feet underground to the compact SIMPol testbed. There, the 6-mm MPG sits in the pupil plane, dividing the wavefront into four pristine polarization portraits of the same sunspot. 

The data reveal interlocking magnetic loops of active regions with clarity matching established instruments, yet acquired without moving parts and at frame rates limited only by the detector.

The metasurface replaces this mechanical system with a single passive optic carved from subwavelength dielectric pillars. These nanopillars impose precise phase and amplitude shifts on orthogonal polarization components, routing nearly 70% of the light into the four science orders, dumping less than 1% into the useless central zero order, and maintaining diattenuation above 98% across channels. The entire polarization analysis collapses into one common optical path and one frozen instant, ideal for space missions where every gram and watt counts. At the Dunn Solar Telescope on Sacramento Peak, its 136-foot tower feeds light 228 feet underground to the compact SIMPol testbed. There, the 6-mm MPG sits in the pupil plane, dividing the wavefront into four pristine polarization portraits of the same sunspot. The data reveal interlocking magnetic loops of active regions with clarity matching established instruments, yet acquired without moving parts and at frame rates limited only by the detector."}}}, "rest_id": "2064800989073522822", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "94", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
ho scritto un sacco di post su questi "photonics" cioè connessioni basate sulla luce o CPO che sono l'ultima moda della speculazione, tipo $Sivers, $Lite, Coherent $COHR Qui vedi finalmente un video di Nvidia che ne mostra un esempio.

 Nvidia video del suo switch di "ottica co-impacchettata" (CPO) con Lambda.

"La corsa all'IA non riguarda solo GPU più potenti, ma anche sprecare molta meno energia mentre quelle GPU comunicano tra loro.

Con l'ottica co-impacchettata (CPO), NVIDIA sta posizionando le parti di comunicazione basate sulla luce molto più vicino al chip di rete principale, invece di collocarli come moduli plug-in separati sul bordo dello switch.

"l'ottica co-impacchettata (CPO) si collega direttamente all'economia dei token. L'energia di rete è un sovraccarico: mantiene le GPU collegate ma non genera token. I guasti di rete sono anch'essi un sovraccarico: trasformano la capacità GPU provisionata in capacità inattiva. La CPO affronta entrambi riducendo il consumo energetico della rete e rimuovendo un'intera classe di componenti ottici pluggable dal fabric.

Un data center con 128.000 GPU che utilizza transceiver pluggable tradizionali richiede circa 655.000 moduli transceiver discreti attraverso il fabric di switching. Ognuno è un potenziale punto di guasto. La CPO rimuove completamente quella classe di componenti.

I carichi di lavoro agentici cambiano la pressione sulla rete. Una richiesta di inferenza tradizionale è relativamente autonoma. Una richiesta agentica può coinvolgere pianificazione, recupero, uso di tool, molteplici chiamate al modello e ragionamento di follow-up. Più dati che si muovono attraverso il cluster. Più punti in cui la latenza di rete o un guasto influenzano il risultato.

L'inferenza multi-agentica richiede un movimento dati elastico e resiliente, in modo che le GPU non aspettino i dati, mantenendo token al secondo e un tempo rapido al primo token."

The AI race is not only about stronger GPUs, but about wasting far less power while those GPUs talk to each other. With co-packaged optics (CPO), NVIDIA is putting the light-based communication parts much closer to the main networking chip, instead of placing them as separate plug-in modules at the edge of the switch. From NVIDIA's official blog on this "co-packaged optics (CPO) connects directly to the token economy. Network power is overhead: it keeps GPUs connected but doesn't generate tokens. Network failures are also overhead: they turn provisioned GPU capacity into idle capacity. CPO addresses both by reducing network power draw and removing a large class of pluggable optical components from the fabric. A 128,000-GPU data center using traditional pluggable transceivers requires roughly 655,000 discrete transceiver modules across the switching fabric. Each one is a potential failure point. CPO removes that component class entirely. Agentic workloads change the pressure on the network. A traditional inference request is relatively self-contained. An agentic request can involve planning, retrieval, tool use, multiple model calls, and follow-up reasoning. More data moving across the cluster. More points where network latency or failure affects the outcome. Multi-agentic inference needs elastic and resilient data movement, so GPUs are not waiting for data, while maintaining tokens per second and fast time to first token.""}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2064706125690462665"}}, "rest_id": "2064769648558756186", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "15345", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2064799993085719023", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1546", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
10 days of $SIVE catalysts. Let's take stock of where we are.

The catalysts:
→ June 2: GlobalFoundries silicon photonics reference design embedment. Stock +50-70%.

→ June 3: Ayar Labs joined NVIDIA's NVLink Fusion ecosystem. EU Tech Sovereignty Package adopted. ATH printed at 110 SEK.

→ June 5: JPMorgan disclosed 5.25% position via Finansinspektionen (transaction date June 2).

→ June 6: $YSS shipped 20+ Tranche 1 Transport Layer satellites for summer Falcon 9 launch.

→ June 8: ALL SPACE awarded $8.2M production order to $SIVE for 2027 Ka-band BFICs.

→ June 9: Anders Storm (former $SIVE CEO) flagged BlackRock and Fidelity as new institutional positions on Morningstar.

The structural conditions tightening underneath:

→ Nordea Bank raised bear-product margins by ~50% in 6 days, citing "poor liquidity in the securities lending market"

→ Three ETF issuers (REX, Defiance, Themes) have 2X LONG $SIVE products filed with SEC

→ MSCI Sweden Small Cap + OMX Stockholm Benchmark inclusions both effective

→ Short interest at 17% of free float

Today the stock is consolidating around 78-80 SEK after the all-time high of 110 SEK on June 3.

That's a ~27% pullback from peak. After a generational run.

This is what healthy consolidation looks like.

5 days until AGM (June 15). Board elections, dilution authorization vote, and management commentary on the Nasdaq NY listing timeline expected.

The chart is consolidating. The thesis is compounding.
$SIVE

其实看完ComputeX有一个很大的感受，就是我的半导体认知还停留在文字，我知道激光器、EML、转换器各种名词，但是当他们真正出现在我眼前的时候。我发现我不认识他们。

不知道小伙伴有没有类似的问题，所以这篇文章将以英伟达的Quantum-X Photonics交换机为例，一起看图说话，看清楚光学交换机里有什么？

text:
Portfolio changed since the last public list.

Main changes: added TEAM/NFLX starters, trimmed IREN, removed NOWL/VIAV/SOXS, and put a tiny SOXL sleeve back on. Also added some NBIS/NVDA/META/HLIT/CEG/ETHA exposure. Translation: still risk-on research, but less "spray and pray semis" than it looks.

My core holdings:

Fintech / software / AI apps — $HOOD, NOW, CRWD, RDDT, PLTR, LMND, TEAM, CRM, NFLX, SG

AI datacenter / compute — NBIS, IREN, NVDA, AMZN, META, HIVE, SANM, OSS

Memory / semis — MU, DRAM, MRAM, TSM, PDFS, CAMT, ONTO, AOSL

Photonics / networking — COHR, LITE, AAOI, GLW, HLIT

AI power — FLNC, CEG, VST, NVTS, ENPH

Crypto / beta sleeves — IBIT, ETHA, XRP, SOXL

Most of my research here is around photonics, memory, AI infra bottlenecks, and power. But the actual portfolio is broader: fintech, software, cyber, consumer internet, and a few tactical sleeves.

The house rule right now is boring on purpose: hold the real proof winners, don't add broad beta just because something got nuked, and only press when the proof or price actually improves.

We'll see which ones earn their way in

I’m buying every major dip on these stocks 

$AAOI — Supplies AI transceivers that convert electrical signals into light for faster data center networking.  
$LITE — Nvidia invested $2B to secure its lasers and photonics for next-generation AI factories.  
$CRDO — Connects AI GPUs with high-speed cables and networking chips, reducing power and latency.  
$DRAM — Memory chips are the fuel for AI; HBM demand continues exploding with Nvidia systems.  
$AMD — Competing with Nvidia in AI accelerators; recently landed major hyperscaler AI deployments.  
$NBIS — Builds & rents out AI cloud infrastructure , they just signed a deal to power France with 240MW
$ASTS — Uses space-based cellular broadband; connecting billions of devices to future space-based AI infrastructure

Think of it like adding more appliances to your house without rewiring anything. You can do it up to a point. Rubin Ultra in 2027 hits that point. 576 GPU dies per rack pulling 600 kilowatts is physically impossible to run on the old wiring. So NVIDIA splits the rack into two separate units. One rack holds all the GPUs. A dedicated sidecar rack next to it handles all the power conversion at 800 volts DC. In the old system a 5kW power supply unit cost around $95 of semiconductor content. In the new 800V sidecar each unit is rated at 30kW and costs around $880 of semiconductor content. The per-unit cost goes up nearly 10x because the chips required to handle 800V cannot be made from regular silicon. You need silicon carbide and gallium nitride, which are more advanced and more expensive materials. Multiply that across an entire rack and you go from $20,186 of power semiconductors per rack at Rubin to $123,092 at Rubin Ultra. That gap does not go to $NVDA. It does not go to the companies building the racks. It goes entirely to the chip companies supplying the power semiconductors. text:
Everything about my investing strategy, capital, and performance since I started investing a year and a half ago.

My investing journey

I started 2025 with around $3.5k, mostly index fund maxxxing.

Then I got wrecked by the Trump market volatility and entered 2026 with around $18.5k and a -5% performance.

Six months later, my portfolio is sitting at roughly $46k . Around $30k came from monthly DCA, but the rest came from performance.

My current goal is to reach $100k by the end of the year while keeping my DCA much lower than in year one. I’m no longer trying to save every penny. Instead, I’m focusing on two things : building a high-conviction bull-growth investing strategy and increasing my income.

I believe the AI supercycle, aka stock market in easy mode, still has a few years left before we return to hard mode.

My strategy is simple : identify businesses positioned to capture a meaningful share of the AI infrastructure buildout, especially in capital-intensive markets where contracts, scale, and execution can quickly reprice a company.

The goal is to maximize returns through short to medium-term asymmetric bets, while holding my highest-conviction names as long as the thesis continues to play out.

Current holdings and 2026 price targets

$IREN : End-of-year PT: $140 (+237%)

IREN is my largest position, representing around 73% of my portfolio.

The stock has underperformed over the last six months, but my conviction remains very high. I believe the next wave of contracts, improved economics, and continued scaling could push the stock significantly higher.

This is my highest-conviction AI infrastructure bet. I plan to hold and watch the company scale until at least 2030, as long as the thesis keeps playing out.

$NUAI : End-of-year PT : $15 (+300%)

NUAI represents around 12% of my portfolio.

The company has built a very strong team and is positioning itself for a potential hyperscaler deal around its 650MW Texas campus.

This single event could potentially double the stock’s value, especially considering the company is currently valued at around $500M .

$SIVE : End-of-year PT : 500 SEK (+500%)

Sivers represents around 10% of my portfolio.

This is a much more speculative position, but the upside could be massive. If Sivers manages to capture a meaningful share of future photonics demand, I don’t see how the company stays below a $3B market cap.

The risk is high, but so is the potential reward.

$AMPG : End-of-year PT : $40 (+500%)

AMPG represents around 5% of my portfolio.

This is my moonshot / for-fun position, built from profits made on a recent swing trade. I haven’t done as much research on this company as I have on my core positions, but the growth potential is serious.

The company could become increasingly involved in contracts with hyperscale clients, which makes it an interesting high-risk, high-reward bet.

Final thoughts

My portfolio is extremely concentrated and clearly not built for everyone.

But I’m young, I have high risk tolerance, and I believe this market still offers many asymmetric opportunities for investors willing to do their D.D and not afraid of volatility.

The objective is to aggressively compound capital during what I believe is the biggest infrastructure buildouts of the century.
